title: SACRAMENTO — Assembly Bill 37, authored by Assemblymember Marc Berman (D-Menlo Park), cleared its final hurdle in the state Legislature today passing out of the Assembly on a 60-17

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Automatically mailing every active registered voter a ballot increases voter turnout.",
    "California had the highest voter turnout in 70 years during the November 2020 General Election.",
    "The emergency election reforms enacted in 2020 resulted in a smoother election process according to elections officials across California.",
    "Every active registered California voter received a vote-by-mail ballot for the 2020 General Election as part of Assembly Bill 860.",
    "Vote-by-mail ballot tracking systems allow voters to ensure their ballots are counted.",
    "Approximately 15 million Californians voted in the General Election using a ballot that was mailed to them.",
    "More than 5 million Californians registered for ballot tracking for the 2020 General Election."
  ]
}
```
